author: xotaki

body: Ok, it seems we can easily fix it. Without cloning repos and building the images manually. We can just define a custom entrypoint for the excalidraw container and overwrite the already built JS assets at `/usr/share/nginx/html/assets` with our custom excalidraw-room url taken from the env `VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL`. Here a working example using Traefik as reverse proxy: ````yaml services: excalidraw: image: excalidraw/excalidraw:latest container_name: excalidraw expose: - 80 restart: unless-stopped entrypoint: /bin/sh command: - -c - | echo "Replacing WebSocket URL with: $$VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL" find /usr/share/nginx/html/assets -type f -name "*.js" -exec sed -i 's|https://oss-collab\.excalidraw\.com|'$$VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL'|g' {} + echo "Starting nginx..." nginx -g 'daemon off;' stdin_open: true healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://127.0.0.1:80/ >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1"] interval: 30s timeout: 5s retries: 3 start_period: 20s environment: - NODE_ENV=production - VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL=https://draw2gether.example.com networks: - proxy labels: - traefik.enable=true - traefik.http.routers.excalidraw.rule=Host(`draw.example.com`) - traefik.http.services.excalidraw.loadbalancer.server.port=80 - traefik.docker.network=proxy excalidraw-room: image: excalidraw/excalidraw-room:latest container_name: excalidraw-room expose: - 80 restart: unless-stopped healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://127.0.0.1:80/ >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1"] interval: 30s timeout: 5s retries: 3 start_period: 20s networks: - proxy labels: - traefik.enable=true - traefik.http.routers.excalidraw-collab.rule=Host(`draw2gether.example.com`) - traefik.http.services.excalidraw-collab.loadbalancer.server.port=80 - traefik.docker.network=proxy networks: proxy: external: true ```` >[!CAUTION] > HTTPS is required due to browser security. Otherwise, collaboration will not work. > Use a reverse proxy and issue proper SSL certificates. Afterwards, collaborations works fine. Just browse `https://draw.example.com` and trigger the `Live collaboration` from the hamburger menu. Share the link and if another person opens it, a new session will occur. Visible at the top right (people icon) as well as interactive mouse pointer with the person's name. Blog post: https://blog.lrvt.de/selfhosting-excalidraw-with-collaboration-support/
author: l4rm4nd

body:
@l4rm4nd how do you keep the Excalidraw server private for self hosting? I tried looking into auth but it seems like there is nothing native in Excalidraw to make the self hosted server private? 
Obviously, I don't want other people to land on my Excalidraw server and use it. 

What's the next best thing to make it private but also be able to use the share feature? 
The share option uses tokens so only the person who got that exact link can then see the document like `https://excalidraw.com/#json=xxxxxx`  
But obviously it should not be blocked by auth. 

I'm curious to hear how you solve this. 

Thanks.
author: codeagencybe

body:
>how do you keep the Excalidraw server private for self hosting? I tried looking into auth but it seems like there is nothing native in Excalidraw to make the self hosted server private?

I personally use Traefik as reverse proxy and Authentik as IdP. 

Traefik can apply middlewares with IP whitelisting or basic authentication. I just use the IdP with forward-auth to enforce an authenticated state/session before being able to access Excalidraw. Alternatively, use internal Traefik entrypoints only and an internal DNS server.

> What's the next best thing to make it private but also be able to use the share feature?

Would require specific reverse proxy rules. The HTTP endpoints for sharing would have to be publicly available. So no middlewares or forward-auth could be applied to these endpoints. 

However, the recipient of the shared link would have to open and be able to access the frontend as well. So either it's public or not I guess.

Have not tinkered with this.

> The share option uses tokens so only the person who got that exact link can then see the document like https://excalidraw.com/#json=xxxxxx

The anker symbol `#` tells the browser to never send anything after it to the backend server. So it remains on the client side, the browser. Therefore, it is not possible to implement rules for anything after `#` on the backend web server.

Just for your information.
author: l4rm4nd

body:
> > how do you keep the Excalidraw server private for self hosting? I tried looking into auth but it seems like there is nothing native in Excalidraw to make the self hosted server private?
> 
> I personally use Traefik as reverse proxy and Authentik as IdP.
> 
> Traefik can apply middlewares with IP whitelisting or basic authentication. I just use the IdP with forward-auth to enforce an authenticated state/session before being able to access Excalidraw. Alternatively, use internal Traefik entrypoints only and an internal DNS server.
> 
> > What's the next best thing to make it private but also be able to use the share feature?
> 
> Would require specific reverse proxy rules. The HTTP endpoints for sharing would have to be publicly available. So no middlewares or forward-auth could be applied to these endpoints.
> 
> However, the recipient of the shared link would have to open and be able to access the frontend as well. So either it's public or not I guess.
> 
> Have not tinkered with this.
> 
> > The share option uses tokens so only the person who got that exact link can then see the document like [excalidraw.com#json=xxxxxx](https://excalidraw.com/#json=xxxxxx)
> 
> The anker symbol `#` tells the browser to never send anything after it to the backend server. So it remains on the client side, the browser. Therefore, it is not possible to implement rules for anything after `#` on the backend web server.
> 
> Just for your information.

I was thinking about doing something like this as well but with Cilium and Gateway API in Kubernetes and then whitelisting only my own app containers to connect to the Excalidraw pods. 
But then it breaks of course the share feature. 

So the next thing I was thinking about, is to use Excalidraw embedded in my application instead. My app has share features build in as well. It's project management/ERP application and uses security tokens to share documents with users. 
So I could embed Excalidraw in my app and then share the record from my app, so the user never has direct access to Excalidraw. Then I can keep Excalidraw completely non-published and only allow my app pods to connect with Excalidraw pods. 
Then I also don't need to worry about the share feature from Excalidraw as I solve it by embedding. 

I was just curious to know if something would be available from Excalidraw directly to avoid all of this, but seems like this is (at this moment) the only viable way to have a private server and being able to share documents.

author: anatoliibakhtin

body:
Hello!

> I've seen #4726 you have already reduced the number of dots in the ink, can this be continued (if that's the reason for the performance to many dots) as now with a large number of lines starts [hangs](https://1drv.ms/v/s!Aug1AU2reREylgkJReSTitEW-4vz?e=Xwqs2U) navigation, new lines appear with a delay, there is no such a thing in google keep. I suppose it could be because:

In your video, the lag you're seeing comes from the fact there are already "too many" lines drawn on canvas which get re-rendered on each pen event as you draw. Simplifying the shape wouldn't help much as we'd still be rendering the scene on each pen movement (it would help only in that it would allow for more elements on scene before you started to notice slowdown) — we'd have to further throttle the pen events (which is where the initial big performance boost came from https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4727).

For further performance improvement we need new rendering strategies and optimizations.
author: dwelle

body:
[@dwelle](https://github.com/dwelle) Thank you! I had no idea how the engine works and all my competence in the code is AutoHotKey scripts on 10 lines BUT I will try to figure it out. 

The strokes [are formed as follows](https://github.com/steveruizok/perfect-freehand#introduction) ? If so (writing for clarification), I see this as a plan to solve this problem:

		  .![рисунки optimize the drawing tool - другой порядок инструментов](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71871141/188129169-3c82ee97-bca4-41b8-9797-54dcfded3ef3.png)

body: This is actually the correct behaviour. The colors are inverted when switching from light to dark mode and vice versa. Hence if you choose a color  lets say "red" in light theme then it will show actual red color but when switching to dark theme, it will  get updated to `lighter` version of `red` so its feasable for dark theme. Similarly for `white` it gets updated to `black` as thats the inverse color for `white` and for `black` the inverse is `white`. Functionality wise the behaviour is correct but UI wise it can create confusion as you mentioned specially for white/black its noticeable. We will check on how we can make this better.

body:
Hi. Yes, the `theme` component prop allows the host app to control the theme, and as such disables the UI button.

If you want to set the theme without making it "controlled", you can use the [`updateScene()`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/packages/excalidraw/README.md#updatescene) API:

```
excalidrawAPI.updateScene({ appState: { theme: "light" } })
```

To set the theme on initial load, set the `appState` in [initialdata](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/packages/excalidraw/README.md#initialdata).

body:
@mfp22 Oke, to identify the issue I need you to go [here](https://jsfiddle.net/cryptix7/6nqckbvs/embedded/result/#Result) with the same browser you've used to access Excalidraw.

This color grid consists of two rows and seven columns, each column showcasing a unique color. The top row uses lossless PNG image, while the bottom row uses CSS-rendered colors, providing a comparative display of the palette's image-file and CSS browser-rendered color representations.

I had an ICC Profile applied in my Window's Colour Management that caused discrepancy in the colors that were rendered from an image-file (top row) compared to the CSS-rendered colors (bottom row), which caused "washed out colors" just like you have described. If you don't see matching colors between the 2 rows, try deleting the default ICC profile and restart the browser, which seemed to fix the issue for me.

If the colors are still washed only in Excalidraw, then you might be using a "dark" theme, in that case that is the intended behavior which is unfortunately not a bug, but a feature. Try using the "light" theme and see if that resolves the washed out color issue.

body:
This issue has been open for years with a working PR that has passing tests. As someone who uses the Excalidraw plugin in Obsidian (which does provide LaTeX support), I can confirm there's real demand and practical value for this feature.

Could you share what's blocking the merge? It would be helpful to understand the specific concerns or requirements that need to be addressed, rather than leaving the community guessing.

Even if there are fundamental issues with the current approach, some transparency about the preferred technical direction would help contributors focus their efforts more effectively.

Thanks for making such an amazing tool!

author: greg-keene

body:
Earlier today I upgraded yarn from classic to the latest version (3.x) in my dev environment for reasons.  I saw the build broken from the same error @rogeti reported above.  I applied the fix @rogeti suggested, but found that eslint was then complaining (incorrectly) about unused imports of types.

I realized this might all be a big mistake, reverted to yarn classic (v1.22.19), but I again saw the same type error regarding TopErrorBoundary, as reported by @greg-keene, even after I deleted node_modules, ran "git reset --hard" and re-ran "yarn; yarn start".   

I then noticed that yarn 3.x had rewritten yarn.lock, and I had accidentally committed the modified yarn.lock to my repository.  The new yarn.lock resulted in the incorrect versions of packages being installed, causing the type error.  After reverting yarn.lock to its previous state and re-running "yarn; yarn start", I was able to resolve this issue.

author: 7flash

body:
Yeah, it's because we invert the colors using CSS, and then individually apply a counter filter on image elements, but it doesn't undo the initial inversion fully.

We could apply the filter on the canvas instead, but there are two problems. (1) The performance is way way worse, to the point it's unusable. (2) canvas filters don't work on Safari (without slow polyfills).

We may improve (1), but I'd have to test the perf to ensure it's acceptable.

(2) is a bigger problem. In fact it already affects Safari where images aren't properly de-inverted as described above, but at least non-image elements are inverted in dark mode.

author: tmcelroy2202

body:
@mchoraine #5311 contains a subtype system for arbitrary extensions of standard Excalidraw elements (`Text`-based or otherwise).  Once we make an `@excalidraw/plugins` package like @ad1992 proposed, you could easily implement your request using the subtype system included in #5311.

I.e. an `ExcalidrawPlantumlElement` would have `type = "text"` and `subtype = "plantuml"`.  Your plantuml plugin could use the `customProps` field of `ExcalidrawPlantumlElement` to switch between different types of diagrams supported by Kroki.  E.g. #5311 uses `customProps` to switch between MathJax input types.

author: soliviantar

body:
@linguax-gc As I have mentioned in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5604#issue-1345533714 , I do not support using Chinese handwriting fonts with embedded font files.

The cost of embedding font files is very high. There are too many CJK fonts, it may take 5M-20M to store and transfer. This will slow down the loading speed of the website. So I choose the option of using a system that includes fonts.

author: soliviantar

body:
> > Incase anyone is wondering, I spent a while figuring this out but I've found a solution.
> > 
> > I've written an article on how to use custom fonts on excalidraw [here](https://dev.to/dawidcodes/how-to-use-a-custom-font-on-excalidrawcom-4jl4)
> 
> Thanks, @DawidWraga, this is working (when using the pastbin you provided). But how do I add the font to the `code` or `handdrawn` font, instead of `normal`. I tried changing it at the beginning of the script and it didn't work. I also tried it on the `font-style` section of the font-face definition with the same result. Looks like it only works on `normal`?
> 
> Also, is it possible to use a non-Google font? Thanks in any case!

Hello @soliviantar 

Sorry if I don't do a great job explaining it in the article I wrote it quickly.

I tried to override the css variables or manually set the font face of the text but that didn't work.

So in the script provided, we're importing a new font and setting the name of this font so that it overrides the default one.

Put another on way,

This didn't work:
Import (new font as new font)
Handdrawn Font family = new font

This did work:
Import (new font as old font)
Handdrawn font family = still the old font
(but now the new font is overriding the old font)

So whether you get the import script from Google fonts or a different cdn it doesn't matter it just matters that you're overriding the correct font family name

For me overriding Helvetica and Virgil worked for normal and handdrawn respectively, but I couldn't get the code font to change (I gave up quite quick as I only wanted to change the normal)

If it's not working on your end it could be because you're in a different OS so there might be a more specific font that is taking presidence 

To solve it id use developer tools and look at what font families are being applied in your browser. Experiment with setting the new fonts name to one of those and see whether it works.

In my attempts, trying to set the css to a new font didn't work so I think taking the route of changing the font definitions name inside the style import is more likely to work.

Let me know if you find a solution for your system and I'll update the article :)

Best of luck!

Thank you, I really appreciate it.

It was 2 days ago but I'll do my best:

  ![Screenshot 2023-05-18 at 10 37 38 PM](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/40357511/5089e00d-fcf0-4f71-b519-980e34d2b0eb)

author: theonlypwner

body:
Google Drive's Realtime API, which powers stuff like Google Docs, uses operational transforms, and their implementation extends it to support undo/redo operations.

They store a stack of local operations along with other collaborators' operations, and when the user undoes a change, it transforms the local operation to undo using the later operations. It is described here about 19 minutes in: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hv14PTbkIs0

Maybe Excalidraw can implement something similar?
author: theonlypwner

body:
To be honest it's a shame that this issue isn't prioritized or *at the very least* put a mitigation in place. We are not talking about a new shiny feature here, we are talking about a problem where **users literally lose hours of work without any way to recover**.

Even something as simple as backing up the drawing every 1 minute would already go a very long way. A global undo would also be a very good first step  that would solve 80% of the problem.

And someone even did a PR that already implements that! It's only 24 lines, it's not rocket science to review…
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6926

Currently we have the worst of both worlds because of what seems to be decision paralysis from the Excalidraw team.

author: DanielJGeiger

body: I'm on Mac and when I copy to clipboard here's how mine turns out looking (blurry). ## 1x Example `width="226" height="134" ` ## Here's my Settings ## Expectation This is how I would expect the image in the clipboard to look: Here's a screenshot at 100% zoom. `width="239" height="142"` I know I'm on High DPI scaling for this screen, and this is a vector based program, but I don't think it's unreasonable to expect it to look clear like this. ## Thoughts Could OS screen scaling be an issue? I still would expect it should export at a 1:1 pixel ratio, at least end up looking smaller if anything, but still clear. I can select a higher scale option like 2x or 3x on the export, but it just results in a larger but still blurry image. ## 2x Example `width="453" height="269"` ## 3x Example `width="680" height="404"` ## Afterthoughts Even the 2x and 3x technically look blurry and not very sharp. I imagine there's some some janky canvas scaling issue happening that's taking the 1:1 pixels and upscaling them to match the screen resolution's higher DPI scaling. Thus to match the screen pixel sizing it's scaling it up which results in a blurry transform. ## Edit I just did a `png` export, when viewed in the system viewer, it's nice and crisp. When I import the file into Google Docs from Google Drive it makes a fairly large image (2563 × 13555) quite blurry. This is a problem of Google's Suite, but the above issue still exists as I pasted directly into this issue from my clipboard. I wanted to leave this finding here in case anyone else is in a similar boat.

author: jimkberry

body:
Ok, I see what has happened - longer ago that I thought! :-)

Anyway, I also have seen the discussions regarding the consequences of separate tabs having separate local storage keys and why folks aren't comfortable making tab states atomic. I think I see a way to make everyone happy, tho - or at least to make it do what I (and the OP, I presume) want without breaking the default tab/drawing sync behavior:

- Introduce the idea of "Scratchpad Mode" for an Excalidraw session (tab.) Once selected, Scratchpad is permanent and irrevocable for that particular session. A session in scratchpad mode has a visible "Scratchpad Mode" bug displayed in the UI, just to make sure folks know.
- When set, scratchpad mode simply causes the app to use `window.sessionStorage` in all places where it currently uses `window.localStorage`.  The 2 APIs are identical, but `sessionStorage` only persists as long as the session and is NOT shared across tabs/windows.

I think that's all of it. I expect that that immediately after the  switch the current state will have to be saved (to sessionStorage this time) before proceeding. - I'd like it best if when I went into scratchpad  mode the tab stayed the way it was when I entered it. There's no cleanup needed since you can't leave the mode. 

There are only 3 source files in the app with references to `window.localStorage` and one in the tests. _[edit: oops, forgot the .tsx files. But still - it's not bad]_

I think I'll take a crack at it in codesandbox and see what happens.
author: jimkberry

body:
Yeah, ok, so not surprisingly I've gotten tied up with other stuff and haven't done anything code-wise for this.

However: I *did* find a workaround which allows you to have multiple simultaneous discrete sessions: just create yourself 3 or 4 browser "profiles" - in Brave anyway setting them up is pretty trivial, as is switching between them and even saving a desktop shortcuts to start a new browser windows with each of them.

This works well enough that I'm not going to try anything further. It also has the added advantage that each separate profile's data is persistent so you do things like having a profile dedicated to a particular "atlas" document and/or set of related libraries - ready to fire up at a moment's notice to use as a source to copy from into whatever you are working on in another session.

author: dwelle

body:
PR #5429 forks and simplifies `@excalidraw/excalidraw` into a stubbed `@excalidraw/plugins`.  If we land a stub package `@excalidraw/plugins`, then we could expand the plugins API thereafter and figure out things like [full|stem|arts].excalidraw.com etc.

The plugins API expansions could include custom subtype extensions of `ExcalidrawElement` and much more.  Custom subtypes would enable a MathJax plugin, UML diagram plugins, "crisp" lines (non hand-drawn) as were requested in Discord, and any other number of plugins which the community might create.

body:
Yeah, agreed that this isn't ideal.

I've checked how other apps do it:

- Miro: no re-selection whatsoever on either redo/undo
- Figma, Tldraw, Affinity: if you deselect or when a shape isn't selected after creation (e.g. for locked tools), and you then create another element which you undo, the previous element isn't selected.

We should align with the latter. Unclear how easy that'll be with our current history model.
author: dwelle

body:
A possibly easy fix is capturing tool selection in history, the downside is the additional undo entry of deselection. I would say it's likely worth the tradeoff, but not super persuaded, so it's up for discussion.

Please check the behaviour in https://excalidraw-git-mrazator-record-unselection-excalidraw.vercel.app/ (to confirm whether it's worth fixing the tests).
author: Mrazator

body:
I think adding an entry each time we change away from the selection tool isn't the way to go as it's adding too much noise into the undo stack — for example when you create 4 rectangles you'll end up with 7 entries as opposed to 4 as is traditional.

Maybe we can add an entry if switching to a tool that has implicit tool lock enabled (in this case, freedraw is such a tool) which doesn't end up in selecting the freedraw shape once you finish drawing it, unlike with not-tool-locked shapes.
author: dwelle

body:
My initial concern was that a similar issue would persist when switching between other tools (i.e. rectangle -> diamond), especially when using shortcuts:

1. Make a rectangle (press 2, draw)
2. Make a diamond (press 3, draw)
3. Realize I've drawn a diamond with the wrong color (oops - panic, chaos) so press Cmd+Z to erase it (instead of changing the color directly)
4. Change the color (oops, I've just changed the color of the previous rectangle instead)
5. Press Cmd+Z to undo the change
6. Make a diamond (press 3, now change the color, draw).

Once you get used to it, it seems obvious to change the color in step 3 immediately, but from my own experience, I've caught myself in this loop multiple times previously, intuitively pressing Cmd+Z when something goes wrong (panic, chaos).

The change above does not make this much better, as the diamond gets unselected (not locked), but it avoids doing steps 4. and 5. 

Anyway, what you proposed sounds even better, though the discrepancy between freedraw (auto-locking) and other tools (switching to selection) might also be slightly confusing.
author: Mrazator

body:
> Once you get used to it, it seems obvious to change the color in step 3 immediately, but from my own experience, I've caught myself in this loop multiple times previously, intuitively pressing Cmd+Z when something goes wrong (panic, chaos).

Though, as you mention, it has a solution that users just need to (and will likely) find out.

With the OP, you can't avoid the extra step.

> Anyway, what you proposed sounds even better, though the discrepancy between freedraw (auto-locking) and other tools (switching to selection) might also be slightly confusing.

Technically there won't be a discrepancy as the step of "deselect the rectangle by changing to freedraw tool, then draw with freedraw, and undo" suggests that undoing should land you to the previous state, which is the rectangle being deselected.

I'd try it, and see!

body: @ummcheng I opened #5108 because over the course of a semester, the boards I taught on stopped saving to Firebase due to deleted elements filling up storage.  It sounds like you're seeing similar symptoms, if for different reasons.  CC @dwelle on the recurrence of scenes failing to save.
author: DanielJGeiger

body: @ummcheng could you share little more context on why filtering out deleted elements is an issue ? We don't save the elements which are deleted and is not updated in last 24 hours.  Are you seeing some inconsistency due to this ?
author: ad1992

body:
Appreciate the prompt attention and help from both of you. 

Yes, it seems like somehow the drawing we have saved in storage has deleted elements that have not been updated in the last 24 hours. On the initial load, all of the elements are included in the calculated scene version, which in this case is `7977`. Then when other changes are made, the scene version calculation excludes those deleted elements (in this case resulting in `6643`, and doesn't attempt to the save the scene, even though the scene is now "newer".
 
I haven't fully traced the lifecycle of deleted elements and how they're updated but I wonder if this is the simplified version of what we experienced:

1. A scene is created (no elements, so calculated version is 0).
2. An element is created and added to the scene (calculated version is 1). The scene is saved.
3. The element is deleted and the scene is still saved to storage since the update time is < 24 hours (version 2).
4. 24 hours later, the scene is loaded and the client calculates the scene version is 2 (including the deleted element).
5. When the save handler is called, the scene version is calculated to be 0 since the deleted element is filtered out, and since the version stored is 2, the save is skipped.

Does that sound plausible at all?
author: ummcheng

body:
> I haven't fully traced the lifecycle of deleted elements and how they're updated but I wonder if this is the simplified version of what we experienced:
> 
> 1. A scene is created (no elements, so calculated version is 0).
> 2. An element is created and added to the scene (calculated version is 1). The scene is saved.
> 3. The element is deleted and the scene is still saved to storage since the update time is < 24 hours (version 2).
> 4. 24 hours later, the scene is loaded and the client calculates the scene version is 2 (including the deleted element).
> 5. When the save handler is called, the scene version is calculated to be 0 since the deleted element is filtered out, and since the version stored is 2, the save is skipped.
> 
> Does that sound plausible at all?

It does sound plausible, and is most like what you're seeing in your project.

This specific problem shouldn't affect excalidraw.com, because we're filtering out the "dead" elements (`getSyncableElements()`) on load before we cache the version:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/3d5356cb8edc043a721a5e66167b2814b191ebc2/src/excalidraw-app/data/firebase.ts#L290-L296

Whereas in your project you're not doing so:

https://github.com/peoplerenaissance/excalidraw/blob/746b55362bf620a5e7041399f64169d0500be94a/src/excalidraw-app/data/httpStorage.ts#L112-L117
author: dwelle

body:
Oh wow, thanks so much for digging in our fork and discovering that! We did remove the encryption functionality and I must have totally missed using `getSyncableElements()` on the initial load.

Thanks again for the help even though this doesn't affect upstream Excalidraw! Will close this issue 🎉

author: dwelle

body:
Copy paste works well on a desktop, thus I would vote for reintroducing the command to the context menu.

I am using an iPad with an external keyboard. Cmd+C, Cmd+V works with text, i.e. editing a text element, I can use the back arrow key+SHIFT to select text and use Cmd+X, Cmd+V to cut and paste; but it does not work with elements, i.e. I select elements on the canvas and press Cmd+C then Cmd+V and nothing happens.
Interestingly Cmd+X works on iPad, but Cmd+V does not paste.

Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V seem to work on an Android tablet with an external keyboard.

Possible root cause - Safari fails to fire paste events (onpaste, onbeforepaste) attached to the document (or body) when Cmd-V is pressed and the focus is not in a text input or text area node.  https://bugs.webkit.org/show_bug.cgi?id=75891
I tried adding `contentEditable={true}`, as suggested in the article, to the canvas and to the canvas of each element, but that did not solve the issue.

For reference here's the PR that removed copy/paste: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/2872

author: ad1992

body: I like the redesign of the points but I don't see why you can't have the bounding box too - it's still helpful for resizing, rotating, etc
author: ajones55555

body:
I agree we should definitely reintroduce not just rotation, but resizing as well as commented above.

Since resizing is also needed, the other non-bounding-box options from https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5535#issuecomment-1205020168 will not solve this.

Question remains, when do we want to show the bounding box? For me I'd do so _at most_ from 3 points and up.
author: dwelle

body:
But even for 3-point lines it may be premature, especially for arrows, because we'll end up with:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/183293126-391a9028-e3e4-473a-a69e-be76eec4bc91.png)

Which means the arrow points will often "collide" with the bounding box handles (though we'd likely make them have preferential hitboxes).
author: dwelle

body:
@dwelle I think 3 points has the first valid use-case for resizing - the only other way to resize would be to move each of the endpoints individually.

Maybe a very small padding could be added to the bounding box? About equal to the radius of the pt handles?

_Sometimes_ a blank canvas loads when a user clicks the excalidraw JSON link.

body: _Sometimes_ a double draw happens when a user clicks the excalidraw JSON link with a used canvas. I've heard this happen with the latest version of Chrome, FireFox, and Brave Browser. This doesn't happen with Incognito windows as far as I know. ### Steps to repro (sometimes): 1. Draw something on excalidraw 2. Click a JSON Excalidraw link such as https://excalidraw.com/#json=AO0CWeSUQb6WD5U4oaqMs,3MJOeSqi6WEpbwULEmLbJA 3. You will be prompted with a dialog `Loading external drawing will replace your existing content. Do you wish to continue?` 4. Click Yes 5. Expectation: The previous canvas will be erased and painted with the JSON content 6. Actual: The previous canvas is not erased && painted with the JSON content

body:
Hello @abs4l. We've reached to you over email. If you haven't received it, feel free to post back to this issue!

That said. There are hacks to "force save", e.g. to refresh the tab, and if it asks for confirmation before leaving, cancel the dialog (stay on page) — that will trigger the autosave immediately. But, autosaving should happen with 1-2 seconds after update, and switching between scenes does autosave immediately.

We will be investigating when and where it may not be happening, though we haven't encountered this issue yet.

author: zsviczian
zsviczian

body:
> I added flushSync to updateScene like this, but that did not resolve the problem. Is this what you had in mind for flushSync?

I'm not sure where the root cause is here, but since I couldn't repro I wanted you to try what you suggested, first.

Honestly, we're having issues with async state updates everywhere, since updating to React 18. Either we start wrapping `setState()` calls into `flushSync` everywhere, or we rearchitect — long term we want to remove react from the editor, so we may as well start that process now.

/cc @ad1992
author: dwelle

body: Is there a way to overload React.Component setState with flushSync included?
author: zsviczian

body:
> Is there a way to overload React.Component setState with flushSync included?

Possibly, for testing. In prod we'd want to create a wrapper method though.

The first step to decoupling react from the editor would be to consolidate updates to a single/couple of dispatches, anyway. Similar to what we do already with `syncActionResult`.
author: dwelle

body: It turns out that the issue I encountered in the afternoon was due to an error in the plugin code, not Excalidraw. I fixed the error, since then I've not experienced issues with React 18. I'll continue to use this version of the plugin for few days. If still nothing, then I will close this issue.

body: @dwelle, just to let you know, in the end, I did modify `updateScene` as proposed [above](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5603#issuecomment-1328263933). The issue happens when I trigger multiple updateScene calls from script using the Obsidian Excalidraw Script API that I developed. Otherwise, all the other issues that I experienced with React18 in August were my coding issues in the plugin, involving the topRightUI JSX component... so nothing to do with the core Excalidraw package.

In general, after a while I see that the context menu suits me (hold and click, not as long as it seems the first time), 

but if you:

-  **add icons next to the description** for example google sheet

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71871141/210762034-9bfaeb32-dc88-4c9a-a9a5-c1a21540ff71.png)

Because a picture is always perceived by the brain first, especially if it is not your native language. Text is a container that shows a picture when we say it out loud or by reading it or by recognizing the lettering of the word, but it takes longer, a picture is more unique, faster, easier

- **add function of pulling your finger** (not two touches, but one with a drag to the side) from the screen to the desired menu item:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71871141/210753902-e2a14195-cc5b-4580-a734-7eb66ea2c822.mp4

It will be faster, easier (sometimes I draw in transport, the phone shakes, I make fewer mistakes when I drag my finger than when I click my finger on the screen ) to select the desired item from the context menu.

body:
I do not know what your concept, not a programmer, looking at how different values appear to disappear on the back of the plugin [obsidian-excalidraw](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin) I can say a few words (hope it will be useful, or  :))
![chrome_f1XnT2WMqf](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71871141/202746753-43a1e041-e24d-4a0b-9b2f-be2e66f93948.jpg)

And once again, the algorithm from the video step by step: 

Blocking:
1. The user selects several items; 
2. The user calls the block items function; 
2.1. The function groups the items by checking if the number of identifiers in the "groupIds" has changed since grouping:
**if yes**, it labels it "fake group"
**if no**, the step is skipped;
2.2. The element is blocked.
I see such options for identifying a group as "fake", maybe some strange ones:  

![chrome_zkxUzVSDug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71871141/202743610-2bb14d18-8ce6-4e7d-bb78-bcd8f9a4508d.jpg)
- **A.**  add another special identifier to the group id;
- **B.** add a parameter which will say fake: true or false.

Unlocking:
1. The user selects the items; 
2. Calls the unlock function: 
2.1. Function checks whether the group is "fake" or not, 
**if yes** unloads, also removing the fake one, 
**if no** skips a step; 
2.2. The function unlocks the items.

p.s. I'm just babbling, picking up options on the surface, not savvy to excalidraw concepts, it seemed necessary to me to do this.
UPD. I probably should have just kept quiet 😅

body:
It could be such a shame if this Twitter voting would stop all future development and improvements of the mobile version. 
As the things are now I think the combination Obsidian + Excalidraw is the most powerful tool created to date ( for thinking, taking notes, knowledge management, visual representation of ideas, studying, sketching in general) (Thanks God @ zsviczian  and the Gods of Excalidraw as well).  I mean, you have all the power of such a tools identically both in mobile and desktop (obsidian at least at the moment), you can make diagrams for your thesis in your home's PC and then when you have to take the bus to school, you can keep doing it on the way without limitations (alias OneNOTE or every Sketching application you could mention), I mean "IT'S INSANE"
Wherever you look there are no tools that are completely cross-platform, there are almost always limits and drawbacks, but with obsidian and Excalidraw it's like I've found the Ultimate tool, I don't even want to think what it would be like if Excalidraw further improved its mobile capabilities, it's as if the only limitation would be your imagination 
I recently discovered these two fantastic tools and no joking, they are changing my life.
I hope developers could reconsider this topic a bit more

Edit:
I would add the issues #5569 and #5570 as well

PD:
Those Twitters who think a mobile version is not necessary, I invite you to try to sketching on the bus with your laptop "living in the third world". When you arrive at your destination you get off the bus without the laptop and without underwear as well D:
author: pikatwinky

body:

author: dwelle

body:
> What's your canvas size? — you can check by righclicking (two-finger tap) on canvas → show stats for nerds.

it was `width:2940px height: 832`


The upper memory limit of the canvas in safari is 384MB.(https://pqina.nl/blog/total-canvas-memory-use-exceeds-the-maximum-limit/)
When zoomed in, the canvas zooms in and waits for the memory limit to be exceeded.

I guess it is the same thing that happens when you zoom in on a small diagram.

I have solved this problem before by developing with canvas.

I can't provide specific code, but...
The problem is that the drawing area that is not visible while zoomed in is eating memory even though it is not visible to the user.

So, if we make only the visible area the canvas, we should be able to minimize the memory usage.
I am not sure if this is the best approach, but it is one way to go.
author: sdw-m-watanabe

body:
> So, if we make only the visible area the canvas, we should be able to minimize the memory usage.
> I am not sure if this is the best approach, but it is one way to go.

What we're doing is we have one main canvas which size is always the `viewport_size * devicePixelRatio`, so on low-dpi-screens where dPR=1, the canvas is always at most the size of your viewport. This is fine.

Where the problem lies is we also create one canvas per each element on the scene as a perf optimization, where we don't rerender the element canvases unless we modify the element or zoom the canvas. But, these element canvases can easily grow beyond viewport size because we need to render them in their entirety per zoom level. We could render just the part that's visible, so their canvases would again be at most viewport size. But then we'd have to recreate & rerender the element canvases on each scroll/move because the visible rendered part would change, which would pretty much negate any perf benefits of this workflow (in fact, it would have even worse perf than not doing this at all).
author: dwelle

body:
I can reproduce a related manifestation on Android mobile (OnePlus 10 Pro 5G, Android 15)

Specific behavior I observed:
- Affects only the freedraw tool ,shapes like rectangles persist correctly at any zoom level
- New strokes are visible while drawing but disappear immediately on finger release at high zoom
- The effect scales with zoom level: the higher the zoom the more strokes disappear
- Zooming back out fully restores normal behavior



Browser comparison on the same Android device:

Chrome (Blink) : bug present
Brave (Blink) : bug present
Free Adblocker Browser (Blink) : bug present
Firefox (Gecko) : works correctly

This suggests the issue is likely related to Blink-based browsers on Android possibly due to differences in how Blink handles canvas rendering or resource limits at high zoom levels compared to Gecko.

To illustrate the difference I’ve recorded two short videos showing the behavior in Firefox and Chrome on the same device.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6beedb2f-4344-4c7e-95f9-2939526383a0

body:
If we consider just the use case you show above, is it an image whose source is coming from outside and thus updates on each load?

For more generic use cases, this is a case for custom, non-canvas-based elements, which is something we want to flesh out more — we're already using this in Excalidraw+ for comment elements.

Or is the requirement for this to be rendered on canvas so you can draw over it? It would need a custom renderer, which may be be fine for simple readonly widgets similar the one you show above, but hard for interactive ones.
author: dwelle

body:
More practically, I am thinking in the context of codebase, - how to keep track over changes in github repository. I would like to paste links to files and have an always up-to-date representation. Here is a first step towards implementation, and the next I want to insert syntax-highlighted images instead of plain text, and then sync those images.

https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/pull/780

author: dwelle

body:
I'm pro human-legible metadata annotations, but think stepping back and considering External Integrations as-such is more appropriate/what this issue is actually about.

Here's how I would imagine an external load feature set fitting together:
1. field & option to load content for a given element from external source, auto updated on change
2. Custom processing for each supported format (pluggable). I.e:
     * Images may have a crop option
     * Drawings may have a group selector
     * Markdown and html would have their own renderers
 3. More complicated extensions could use intermediate files to supply richness needs or intermediate representations, ie the above weather plugin would just produce an html snippet, something really complex like an always up-to-date code dependency graph plugin would probably generate per-file markdown summaries, then generate a drawing from that or something.

author: micimize

body:
How about implementation of traditional Spreadsheet/Excel formulas but assignable to elements rather than cells? 

When selecting any element, it can show an island component with input field to enter formula, which can reference functions and other elements, and when pressed Enter the formula is executed, replacing text of the element with returned value of the function, and still remembering the formula in element.customData.fx

`weather("SF")` will execute async function defined in window.weather passing ElementID and "SF" as arguments

But what if you want a formula to dynamically take "SF" value from another text element? In spreadsheets, you can reference cells by their coordinates in XY axis where X are alphabetic letters and Y are incrementing numbers. In difference, elements cannot be referenced by coordinates, and also it's not practical referencing them by ID.

Instead, we can reference elements by prefix matching, that's so we can create a text element "City: SF" within same group with our output element containing formula `weather(el("City: "))` which would work same as before but when you change city in referenced element, it can automatically re-executed weather function and update output element.
author: 7flash

body:
> If we consider just the use case you show above, is it an image whose source is coming from outside and thus updates on each load?
> 
> For more generic use cases, this is a case for custom, non-canvas-based elements, which is something we want to flesh out more — we're already using this in Excalidraw+ for comment elements.
> 
> Or is the requirement for this to be rendered on canvas so you can draw over it? It would need a custom renderer, which may be be fine for simple readonly widgets similar the one you show above, but hard for interactive ones.

It's a text coming from outside and then rendered as html on the offsite canvas, exported to PNG and then added as an image to the actual canvas. This approach allows to draw over it and move it around, yet refresh it by re-rendering source code and just replacing the image at any time.

body:
This would be great for folks who use excalidraw for work, and who work on multiple projects.

I have one collection per project I work on. I have different teammates collaborating with me on each project.

Technically, teammates collaborating with me on project A should not be able to see diagrams I have created for project B, and vice versa.

Other options:
1. Allow me to set a whole collection to Private (in addition to the pre-existing Private collection).
2. Allow me to move a whole collection into the pre-existing Private collection - IOW, I could move COLLECTION A and COLLECTION B into Private and the scenes in each collection would remain grouped with each other but would not be visible to collaborators.
author: kurtmilam

body:
> Allow me to set a whole collection to Private (in addition to the pre-existing Private collection).
>
> Allow me to move a whole collection into the pre-existing Private collection - IOW, I could move COLLECTION A and COLLECTION B into Private and the scenes in each collection would remain grouped with each other but would not be visible to collaborators.

Moving whole collections that we public to become private has certain implications we don't know how to solve right now, namely, whether you should be able to make private the scenes which you did not create.

If the goal is rather to have nested collections/folder, that will come.

body:
This would be really nice to have this! ctrl + scroll was made to differentiate scrolling from zooming in linear ui / documents where you navigate primary by scrolling up and down, it doesn't really make sense on a canvas where you can just use the scroll wheel to point and zoom (1 hand vs 2 before) or can move around with keyboard directional arrows (1 hand vs 2 before) or by holding the middle or right click while moving the mouse ( still 1 hand vs 2 before)

Most modern whiteboard apps (Miro / heptabase / Mural..)  give the option to change between what they call trackpad navigation (zoom w ctrl + scroll wheel) and mouse navigation (point and zoom)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/51007222/206908003-97371776-477c-44f3-a849-4ca97a1bcf67.png)

Not strictly against making the scroll as the default, or adding navigation types.

Problem with making it the default is that many people don't know how to pan the canvas (they are either not used to `mousewheel-drag` or don't read the hints). So we'd at minimum need to wait until we introduce the panning tool.

body:
OK dug around a bit more and it looks like its some combo of WSL2, Docker and windows 11 that's causing this. 

Greenshot (the screenshot tool) also has an issue on this: https://github.com/greenshot/greenshot/issues/348 which they seem to have resolved by using a different way to copy to windows' clipboard. Recap here: https://github.com/greenshot/greenshot/issues/348#issuecomment-1033470769

But the core problem seems to be in WSL's GUI layer as I found via this issue here: https://github.com/microsoft/wslg/issues/443

I will try the fixes mention and get back. But as of now, this is **not** an excalidraw related issue. It seems like excalidraw was just in the line of fire, sorry. I hope we can keep this open until resolved from my end, so that it can be a reference for passerby like me.
author: shrayasr

body:
Okkkk. This seems to be fixed now. Just adding the solution here for clarity: 

I tried to do a `wsl --update` as advised [here](https://github.com/microsoft/wslg/issues/443#issuecomment-1156128086) but this didn't fix it. Looks like I was on an older version of WSLg (the GUI layer) since as per their instruction `wsl --version` gave me an error. 

Then I installed WSL preview from here: https://aka.ms/wslstorepage and it seems to have been fixed now when copying an image from both excalidraw and photoshop 

My `wsl --version` now shows 

```
WSL version: 0.70.0.0
Kernel version: 5.15.68.1
WSLg version: 1.0.45
MSRDC version: 1.2.3575
Direct3D version: 1.606.4
DXCore version: 10.0.25131.1002-220531-1700.rs-onecore-base2-hyp
Windows version: 10.0.22000.1098
```

Closing this. Thanks for your assistance folks! :)

author: dwelle

body:
May I work on it? 
If I understand well the behaviour should be: 

- If clipboard has a text with multple lines, inform the user with a toast that the text can be copied as single splitted lines
- ```CmdOrCltr+Shift+V``` the element is copied splitted.

Should the behaviour be also implemented for copy/paste of text element:

- Clipboard has a text element
- After the user copied a text element a toast should inform the user that there's the possibility to ```CmdOrCltr+Shift+V``` to paste the splitted text element
- ```CmdOrCltr+Shift+V``` the element is copied splitted.
author: adarkforce

body:
@adarkforce sure thing!

> If I understand well the behaviour should be:
> 
> * If clipboard has a text with multple lines, inform the user with a toast that the text can be copied as single splitted lines
> * `CmdOrCltr+Shift+V` the element is copied splitted.

Not quite. We'd split into multiple elements automatically. The toast would only say that to not split into multiple lines, you can either paste into an existing text element (editor), or use `Cmd-Shift-V`.

Refer to the below diagram:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197391175-d3dd8ede-e11d-402c-85c3-a0998399cd54.png)

> Should the behaviour be also implemented for copy/paste of text element:

No. When coping text elements, it copies a JSON to the clipboard, not text. The JSON will be interpreted as non-plaintext and handled in (4.2) — as it should, because you want to duplicate the element, not reinterpret the text.

body:
I've pushed some tweaks and removed the async clipboard API as it's not needed, see comment https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5786#issuecomment-1288209760.

What we should decide is how much to space out the text elements, and whether to center them:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197420151-e643338f-3bfb-4009-92d4-bb80ff71ce4a.png)

(1) is single text, (2) is current PR's multi-element text, which visually aligns with (1). I'd also consider (3) for easier manipulation, and (4) based on what we think will be the use case.

Why (3): because the elements are so close together that the bounding boxes overlap:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197420211-f7174c95-900c-45d5-8ef0-48c59e936a8f.png)
author: dwelle

I think that spacing them would be better for accessibility. 

The text centering emphasizes the separation so I think that is more beautiful, but it also depends on the use case. 

I would vote for 4.

body: Could this be a issue with Safari's canvas implementation? Or am I missing something here?
author: LukaHietala

body: Ultimately we're running into memory issues from a certain point. More a problem for exporting to PNG than during editing, unless individual shapes' size get above a certain point, because right now we're caching each shape to a `(width*zoom) x (height*zoom)` canvas to improve rendering performance. That means if the product of those values gets above max allowed canvas size on a given browser (in Obsidian's case, that's Chromium), it will either crash or fail to render. Or be slow / fill up memory if close to the limit.
author: dwelle

body: I am not sure I understand how this explains the problem of freezing. iPads freeze when the user zooms out, i.e. the zoom multiplier is typically small in the range of 0.1-0.5. However, the product of the many small elements could add up. I can better understand the memory explanation when zooming in on an image and the image disappears after a certain point. In this case, I can see that the cache size for the image increases as the zoom level increases, and when it hits a size threshold the image is not cached. However, this is a seemingly different issue.

author: sir-charles90er

body:
@sir-charles90er, I am sorry to hear that.
I've not experienced the freedraw, iPAD freeze issue, since well over a year now, thus I conclude the original issue was resolved.
That said, Excalidraw is not a handwritten notes tool. It is an illustration tool that can handle some volume of scribbles. I do not plan to optimize Obsidian-Excalidraw to compete with tools like notability.

Sometimes other plugins impact the performance of Excalidraw in Obsidian. This is because Excalidraw notes tend to become large and some plugins index, process markdown notes in the background, which becomes a major performance problem when Excalidraw files get big. I recommend reviewing these two pages and apply solutions to improve on your situation:
- https://excalidraw-obsidian.online/wiki/troubleshooting/performance
- https://excalidraw-obsidian.online/wiki/troubleshooting/compatibility

Finally, this is the Excalidraw issue log, not the Excalidraw-Obsidian issue log. Your issue seems to be related to Obsidian. 
https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues : However, as said, this is not a feature I plan to work on. Sorry for that.

body:
> PS: this line in docker-compose didn't fix it:
> 
> ```yaml
>     environment:
>       - REACT_APP_LIBRARY_URL=https://libraries.excalidraw.com
> ```

@artemanufrij That won't work because the docker-compose.yaml uses Dockerfile to build the app and you have to pass the env variables through build args and then reference them in the Dockerfile. 

Also you won't be able to set env variables on the image you pull from Dockerfile as excalidraw uses `create-react-app` which builds a static build with env variables baked in, which you can't update.

I think this will need to be changed in this repo. Let me create a PR and hopefully it will get merged quickly.

author: amitnovick

body:
To my understanding, this is not an easy task. For the free users, storing data locally on browser is not a big concern because these users are typically not working on huge volume of scenes. They also do not store sensitive data.

But for plus users, synchronizing data from offline storage to the remote server is tricky. Imagine if you are using excalidraw on a public computer library: once you log in to your account, you do not want previous offline users' data to be synced to your account. Similarly, when you are offline (either temporarily or on a serious network error), you would not want your private data to be transferred to the offline browser storage, which can be inspected by the next users.
author: sindras

body: That being said, it is not impossible. One example web app that I can think of is gmail's [offline mode](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/1306849). It provides security by encrypting the offline data with the credentials of the last logged in account before the connection went out.
author: sindras

author: DanielJGeiger

body: The empty `text`/`originalText` sets `maxWidth` nearly to `0`.  This causes text wrapping to calculate a height based on single-character widths once `text`/`originalText` are no longer empty.  But I'm not sure why they are initially empty or when they become non-empty.

body: @eproxus I wonder if we should fix this, as what you're undoubtedly trying to achieve here is to have a bounded text area for wrapping without the downside of using a rectangle container. Support for this will be implement in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4267.
author: dwelle

body:
@dwelle Great to see a more fleshed out feature, although I don’t immediately see the difference between that and a normal box without a border. I’d rather have a text flow setting for the normal boxes that a completely different tool (unless I’m missing something that makes a separate tool needed). 

I only outlined one example usage where the current behavior was surprising. I’d argue that setting text color to the same as stroke color when changing it breaks the principle of least surprise no matter the intention of the user. Especially since there is a separate setting for text color.
author: eproxus

body:
> I’d rather have a text flow setting for the normal boxes that a completely different tool (unless I’m missing something that makes a separate tool needed).

There won't be a new tool. It will work like that with the current text tool if you draw a rectangle with it, instead of clicking.

> I only outlined one example usage where the current behavior was surprising. I’d argue that setting text color to the same as stroke color when changing it breaks the principle of least surprise no matter the intention of the user. Especially since there is a separate setting for text color.

That's intentional so it's easy to quickly change the rectangle+text together, which I'd think is the more common use case.

Setting the container to transparent stroke should def be the edge case that shouldn't require this hack, but rather be solved with the text area thing outlined above.
author: dwelle

body:
> That's intentional so it's easy to quickly change the rectangle+text together, which I'd think is the more common use case.

@dwelle I personally don’t agree with that assumption. As soon as you have selected a different text color individually I’d argue you never want this behavior.
author: eproxus

body:
I tend to agree with @eproxus 
I use Excalidraw every day and use containers very frequently to wrap text. Having a transparent border color for the container and a different stroke color for the text is the most frequent use case when working with sticky notes. In the Obsidian-Excalidraw implementation, I have a script that solves this for me... but it would be even nicer if I did not need a special script to achieve this behavior.
author: zsviczian

body:
>  Having a transparent border color for the container 

Right, but as I suggested above, I assume this is to handle the missing text-area/wrapping behavior of the text tool? 

>  different stroke color for the text is the most frequent use case when working with sticky notes

For sticky notes, yes. But for simple container-labeled shapes, in my experience, people want to have the same stroke for the container as for the text.

So the question is, should we have a separate tool to create sticky notes as many other apps do? At first we wanted to do so, but decided against it to keep the toolbar simple.
author: dwelle

body:
@dwelle Let's just agree to disagree then. I know of no other tool that sets text color when stroke color is modified (but not the other way around, strangely enough). I think it's terribly inconsistent.

I personally prefer as few tools as possible, if the effect can be achieved with a flexible base tool. In this case, the tool is not so flexible and generic as I'd like.
author: eproxus

body: I often have a similar need: I want to draw a rectangle with some text inside, a background color, and **no** border, which, as far as I know, is not possible today without drawing the rectangle (with a transparent stroke color) and the text separately. I'm not sure we should have a separate color for the stroke and the text. I'm wondering if a simpler solution is not to add the possibility of having a zero/none stroke width. That would also solve the use case of drawing rectangles without borders in a more "discoverable"/obvious way.
author: ngrilly

body:
> I often have a similar need: I want to draw a rectangle with some text inside, a background color, and **no** border, which, as far as I know, is not possible today without drawing the rectangle

It is, but somewhat clumsy: 1) change stroke of both 2) start editing text and change the color of the text while editing it.

I'm sure there are many other people to whom it won't be immediately obvious you can do this — which is why if we decouple the container & text colors, they won't then know how to change the text color.

---

> I'm wondering if a simpler solution is not to add the possibility of having a zero/none stroke width. That would also solve the use case of drawing rectangles without borders in a more "discoverable"/obvious way.

We would then have two different mechanisms: 1) for background, to disable it, you pick a transparent color. This also hides the background fill types. 2) for stroke, to disable it, you'd pick 0 width stroke.

Also, what if we want to later support text stroke width? We'd have the same issue as with color.

---

If we did want to decouple the container & text stroke colors, then one way to do it is show 2 color pickers for each, and perhaps separate the properties into 3 groups:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/205972438-6040bfd6-0e23-48fa-80dc-f5e5058e37f6.png)

And the problem is I still think the common case is people wanting to edit container & text stroke color together.
author: dwelle

body:
> I'm sure there are many other people to whom it won't be immediately obvious you can do this

Actually, I now remember I did this in the past, but I forgot! 😅

> if we decouple the container & text colors, they won't then know how to change the text color.

That would be great.

> We would then have two different mechanisms: 1) for background, to disable it, you pick a transparent color. This also hides the background fill types. 2) for stroke, to disable it, you'd pick 0 width stroke.
> 
> Also, what if we want to later support text stroke width? We'd have the same issue as with color.

I agree that's not a good design.

> The problem is I still think the common case is people wanting to edit container & text stroke color together.

Good point. Not sure how to solve this... Maybe the default is to keep the stroke and the text colours in sync, and "decouple" them only when the text colour is explicitly modified?... Just brainstorming here.
author: ngrilly

body:
> If we did want to decouple the container & text stroke colors, then one way to do it is show 2 color pickers for each, and perhaps separate the properties into 3 groups:

I'm happy with this approach.

If you want the default behavior to be that it matches, you could still have that. Just have it say `match` or something in field for text color. Then it will pick up the stroke color by default, but if you change it, it will be come independent.

author: indrajitbnikam

body:
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6717 seems stuck on the issue of the proposed solution's alignment w/ Excalidraw's design principles.

so perhaps a fresh take w/ that in consideration:

### stated problem (refocused)
we have a shape (eg. box) w/ some text inside. we want to make the shape stroke color transparent, while keeping the text inside stroke color unchanged, but when we select the shape and set the stroke color to transparent it is applied to both.

we can followup and set the text inside stroke color directly to workaround, _but when we need to do this to many elements at once in batch then it becomes a serious time-sink_ ⏳.

### guiding principle (i presume)
if we're considering the text inside as child of the shape, this behavior is actually consistent w/ what we expect to happen when doing the same to a _grouped set of elements_ (ie. the change is applied to all elements within hierarchy)

### 🖼️ actual problem (reframed)
we cannot select only the parent container of shape to apply changes only to that and not to the text inside as well. 

### 🧪 possible solution to actual problem
we could add a context menu item to **"Select parent(s)"** to reduce selection to only the root element(s).

this could then work the same for a _grouped set of elements_.

likewise we can have a context menu item to **"Select children"** to do the inverse and reduce selection to only leaf element(s).

### result

this could, in general, unlock an effective workflow for _applying hierarchically-targeted changes, especially in batch_.
author: json2d

body:
@gustavindnq, this feature is there, but in a slightly different way.
- You can set a default for this in plugin settings
- You can override that setting by adding the following frontmatter keys to your drawing in markdown view mode. For more please consult the [Readme](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/blob/master/README.md).
  - Frontmatter tags to customize image export at a file level [519](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/519). If these keys are present they will override the default excalidraw embed and export settings.
    - `excalidraw-export-transparent: true`:  true == Transparent / false == with background. 
    - `excalidraw-export-dark`: true == Dark mode / false == light mode.
    - `excalidraw-export-padding`: Specify the export padding for the image
    - `excalidraw-export-pngscale`: This only affects export to PNG. Specify the export scale for the image. The typical range is between 0.5 and 5, but you can experiment with other values as well.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/202917790-91c066b1-271c-495a-bc17-a0bb4afa95d4.png)

body:
The custom sidebar is closed by default. You should either open it on a user action, using the api [toggleMenu()](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/packages/excalidraw/README.md#toggleMenu) API: 

```
<button onClick={() => excalidrawAPI?.toggleMenu("customSidebar")}>
  Toggle Custom Sidebar
</button>
```

Check the example app: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/packages/excalidraw/example/App.tsx#L684

Or, if you want to have the sidebar open from the start, set `initialData.appState.openSidebar` to `"customSidebar"`.

I would not break the current behavior, especially if we're keeping the double-click behavior.

If the concern is UX, with which I agree, then let's instead highlight the element which we'd bind to:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/202138199-5681c8a6-8d77-4431-b4f8-5ec2ea49ed3f.png)

author: dwelle

body:
If you are talking about "double click on center only" to bind, I think that's something we should think about since that's not intuitive as well. If we want to keep double click then it should be the entire container and not just the center.

As per hover, that's something we should add to make it more intuitive but again I don't think keeping it restricted to just "Center" when a double click is intuitive even if we highlight so I think we should make it a whole container instead. 

Also, this would align with other tools and for "Arrow with labels" we are enabling double click on the whole container. 
And talking about the "text tool" I would want to keep it independent of binding for better intuitive behavior and simplicity, I am sure people either don't use it for binding or will be surprised if it binds.

If we still want to allow the text tool then it should be for the whole container instead when we highlight, and the same goes for double click as well

author: maielo

body:
I'd withhold that decision until we start highlighting hovered elements.

IMO the text tool creating bound text in the center of the element is actually less controversial (more intuitive) than dblclick (which we want to keep). We can highlight the element when hovering over center of an element with the text tool. With dblclick we can't do that.
author: dwelle

body: Ad the "what other apps do": I'm generally very biased towards aligning with other apps. But we also need to consider the context of those apps, and what features they do or don't support, and what their target use case is. And lastly, we also need to consider whether breaking the standard behavior isn't the way to go in order to improve UX.

author: jauco

body:
Answered in https://github.com/preactjs/preact/issues/3809 but responding here for visibility:

The main issue is that the `@excalidraw/excalidraw` package ships with a partially inlined version of `react` and `react-dom`. Looks like the external webpack setting is not sufficient to exclude those. The currently inlined netries are:

- `react/jsx-runtime`
- `react-dom/client`

With explicitly excluding entries defined in React's `exports` field in `package.json` I was able to ensure that these are excluded from the dist files:

```diff
// webpack.config.js
externals: {
    react: {
      root: "React",
      commonjs2: "react",
      commonjs: "react",
      amd: "react",
    },
    "react-dom": {
      root: "ReactDOM",
      commonjs2: "react-dom",
      commonjs: "react-dom",
      amd: "react-dom",
    },
+    "react-dom/client": {
+      root: "ReactDOMClient",
+      commonjs2: "react-dom/client",
+      commonjs: "react-dom/client",
+      amd: "react-dom/client",
+    },
+    "react/jsx-runtime": {
+      root: "ReactJSXRuntime",
+      commonjs2: "react/jsx-runtime",
+      commonjs: "react/jsx-runtime",
+      amd: "react/jsx-runtime",
+    },
  },
```

author: ghost

body: @imvetri This is not a Preact issue. This error is likely coming from the usage of private class properties in the excalidraw code. The log file you posted contains a few classes similar to this one: Based on that my assumption is that either: 1. They are not supposed to be there and expected to be transpiled away. This would mean that the excalidraw files were generated with the wrong target environment in their build step. 2. They are supposed to be there, in which case it would indicate that the target environment you're trying to run in is too old and doesn't support private class properties. Either scenario is not a Preact issue and I'm not involved with the excalidraw project to know what the intended path is.

author: dwelle

body:
Hello. We will be working on redesigning the current color picker soon. I'll create an issue with details to discuss tomorrow.

That said, at current time we are not planning to implement a full color picker in the sense you mean (a color wheel) — mainly because at Excalidraw our goal is to keep it both simple and with as little friction as possible. While that may sound counterintuitive (forcing you to go pick a color from elsewhere), it's about not overwhelming you with choice (decision paralysis), and promoting consistency. We'll got into this in detail in a blog post later (and I'll give some more detail in the follow-up issue).

I'll keep this open for now so I remember to link this issue.
author: dwelle

body:
I understand and agree with you that keeping it simple is the right choice. My suggestion is as such for the text input where the selected color appears. Usually these inputs with colors, their main function is to paste the color that one wants or copy the one that is there.

But in case you want to modify them, making them by hand is very difficult because the color that is being selected is not known for sure. My suggestion comes from this problem. 

Suppose I select a color from the Excalidraw palette, but I want to clarify it a bit. This is almost impossible for me to do by hand in that input, I end up opening Photoshop pasting the color and modifying it there for back with that color to excalidraw. 

Maybe using an input `type="color"` or a third party lib for this over the input would improve the UX.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/53962116/204909935-c4f0fb5f-2b7c-4c12-bdef-223f37aee555.png)

Again, this is just a suggestion and I understand if you just decide to leave it as it's. I hope it can be of use.

Thanks for this great tool :)
Regards.
author: manuelernestog

body:
Forgot to link the proposal for the new color picker redesign I mentioned. There are some points on why we don't want to go with a full color picker for now, and a proposal for adding color shades that you might find useful enough as substitute — at least that's goal, since Excalidraw shouldn't be about perfection and exactness. That said, as mentioned in the issue the doors to adding that full color picker are still not closed in the future.

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5931

Closing in favor of the above issue, so please feel welcome to discuss more over there!
