author: ad1992

body: Hello ! You have a discussion here, I am happy to offer my options. I'll start from the outside-in order 1-9, answering your ideas (!), questions (?), considering (#) wishes. I hope your time will not be wasted, I was interested to try to organize it all on a sheet, using your tool and the excalidraw-obsidian plugin. Plan: # 1. #4086 (The keyboard shortcut prompts are not everywhere) Add the "s,g" designations there — good idea. # 2. idea 3, 4 (using different colors for canvas background, fills, strokes): If you change only brightness (not saturation or hue), you get **1**, but if you also change saturation, you get **2** more contrasty, readable, less juicy. I would choose the 2nd option, but not knowing about 2 I would love to use 1. Picked up color saturation based on samples from [google cursive](https://cursive.apps.chrome/) # 3. idea 5 ( move the section "colors used on the canvas" to main menu) For me, this means the user's color is more important than the preset. It seems it might break the color style of the whole board. Drop by drop the mess will appear. If you do want to move the section "colors used on the canvas", to the main menu, I like options 2, 3 (2 is a little harder to hit with your fingers than 3) I would leave the layout unchanged, changing the color selection concept: The concept — pressing the S or G key pops up a color selection menu (as before or on the side), the cursor turns into a dropper, while pointing such a dropper at any button or tool, it is replaced by the cursor and back dropper if focus is lost. # 4. question 1 (how to make the button on color selection even more obvious) Remove the frame around frequently used colors so that they do not look like a button (let the rule of your program will be everything in the frame — a button — can be pressed). # 5. Should the colors used on the canvas be removed from the popup menu? I think so, all information that is duplicated should disappear. # 6. idea 2, question 3 (default color), (what are the base colors). Yes I would like other colors. In 6 months of using excalidraw I only used these colors (the ones with the green dot) as the others were too similar to each other, i.e. in fact the available to me were 6 groups— 10 colors. ![download](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71871141/205437054-c4f44688-b211-45f3-8241-dc16ebe7be42.svg) I was using google keep before, and after observing myself I made a conclusion, I used gray-black colors most often, + a few colors, to highlight, denote objects: important, ready, grass, water, fire., so I would like to have in excalidraw such a scheme: I took it from google cursive, reworked it a bit, black 3, 1 white, 1 transparent (#4842), colored 7x3 (almost like in a rainbow, so when drawing you can identify the object on the canvas with an object from real life, separate, set up a hierarchy), total of 25 + 1 transparent. Now I think — no more is needed (13x5 is too much, will take up space, I think so). # 7. idea 1 (few colors to choose from, way to expand) Shadow option — cool idea! I would reduce them from 5 to 3, build them into new palette. # 8. question 2 (would you save shade position when you change color for another color) If my palette is 7x3— no need save shade position, each hue is a separate felt-tip (press S, choose color); if your palette is 13x5— yes, you should save, because by choosing another hue you kind of choose another ink mode. # 9. question 4 (whether to keep the ability to change the color) Yes, the user should be able to choose the color. Sometimes this is what the customer or my inner artist wants :). The palette itself will look like this: at the very top is the name of the mode in which the palette works, more details below. The components of the palette are arranged in 16 cells: - 1-J— can change, depending on the palette mode (there is an internal rule №1— **1** cells are always the darkest, most saturated, **J** the lightest, faded); - Z-B— never change; - N— dropper (#5582): select objects, press N or on button, cursor changes to dropper, point it to pop up color palette, or object on canvas, or behind canvas (in other program), click (if you dragged cursor from dropper release mouse), objects will paint completely (stroke, fill) in 1 color, according to idea 3,4 (point 2 of current messag). There are only 2 droppers, 1 for color selection, the other for complete repainting of the object; - M— color palette settings: 1. M1— its mode of operation (click to activate, inactive options become grayed out so as not to attract attention). Palette modes: - **St**andard— you can change 1-7 cells, Q-J (see internal rule №1) — are auto-selectable tones for the hue above; - **A**nalogous—Initially the same colors as Standart, but only cell 1 can be changed, everything else will be picked up automatically according to internal Rule №1, except for line 1-7 changes to the right according to the next color tone, not the saturation (Hue) - **M**onochromatic— only the center element "r" can be changed, the rest will be picked up according to rule №1; - **C**omplementary, **S**plit **C**omplementary, **D**ouble **S**plit Complementary, **T**riad, **Sq**uare: you can change any single cell, the rest will be selected on the base of their scheme and rule №1; - **Cu**stom— all rules are disabled, cells are just containers for color. The frame is intentionally made bright black for demonstration only, in the working variant it will be as point 9 of current messag, barely visible (image above) 2. M2— dumps all colors to default settings, sets the palette to Standard; 3. M3— import palette; 4. M4— export the current palette (#3643). To summarize, the palette looks like this: The old mode of arrangement of elements in the main menu; added s/g hint; new concept of color selection; different modes of palette operation; new color scheme; dropper tool for full recoloring, considering the principle "background, fill, outline"; palette settings: mode, reset, import, export; used colors on the canvas. Horizontal or vertical. #5872 🤷‍♂️ UPD: there is a plus to leave the field for entering a color value wide, that way I edit colors taken by pipette from [PowerToys](https://github.com/microsoft/PowerToys) mode HSL. Although the font size itself could be reduced to make it all fit. ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71871141/205664894-6173f9d9-e3f9-42c1-8a5b-9f6c1f5b7c8c.png) p.s. Take this message as options/a set of ideas, but not as a requirement. Thank you for your attention ! 🌼

body: The new color picker is looking great. I like the shades feature. It would still be great to be able to select a color with a full color picker (or to be able to sample one from on screen/canvas). author: andyfleming

body:
@andyfleming we will definitely be adding an "eye dropper" to support sampling colors from the canvas.

But we want to avoid a full color picker for now to retain simplicity, reduce decision paralysis, and promote consistency!
author: dwelle

body:
> @andyfleming we will definitely be adding an "eye dropper" to support sampling colors from the canvas.

Oh, perfect. That, combined with the color shade options would ease things a lot for me.

> But we want to avoid a full color picker for now to retain simplicity, reduce decision paralysis, and promote consistency!

Understandable. I think the swatches and shade features do that. Why even let someone type in a hex color then? (PLEASE DON'T TAKE THAT AWAY 😄 )

I think at the point that they are setting a custom color, they are already going out of their way to do something custom. Having a flexible picker available doesn't compromise that. I'm not suggesting you make it the first thing that comes up; just that it's available _somewhere_. Plus it would give you the eye dropper feature for "free".

body:
I tried playing with this and yes I can also notice the same issue, here are some points that I saw: 

- All these issues are only clearly visible with 500% or higher zoom, the effects can be seen at 300% as well but the deformation is minimal. 
- Edge points for the diamond are re-calculated based on a certain interval of re-sizing to make sure it's a perfect diamond, but in between those steps is where the diamond loses its shape.
- Edges touching the right and bottom corner seem to be affected, the top left edge is always in good shape (noticed with rounded corners, not very evident with sharp corners)

Possible Solution 
The interval for re-calculating the edge points looks to be based on the canvas size, making it dependent on screen size or change in shape with respect to the screen would fix it but might bring other problems I can't think of right now

body:
Thanks! It looks like an issue with pointer pressure. On Android it emits touch events with pressure, where on iOS it does not. Perhaps we should ignore pressure from touch events?

https://excalidraw.com/#json=iOrxaaIJ0WKLJ5-9iIqP8,JbQ-fTkGKVNQuFL5_ISraw
author: dwelle

body:
Yeah, it emits 0.5 on Android and 0 on iOS for `event.pressure`.  Currently, it looks like the following condition determines whether the pressure is "real" or "fake": `      simulatePressure: event.pressure === 0.5,` ([link to code](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/components/App.tsx#L4083))

I guess an additional check for `0` value there will do the trick, as long as we can safely assume that "real" pressure from things like Apple Pencil is always > 0.

body: I would suggest this idea (6) with an explanation of the design change: Black, white, argue with each other, causing doubt as to which one is the main one, so there is difficulty in understanding how these buttons work. If you color them, doubts remain if you don't know which color is the accent color. Because of this, I put even more emphasis on the main element with the scale. But I don't like how it looks "at the very bottom", too "noisy". The lowest or the highest usually marks the border of the canvas, so I replaced the square with a silhouette. If we have border markings on the left and right I would change their placement to this: Step 6 to center the figures not on the accent square, but on the whole group: Thanks for your attention! **UPD:** I will attach all options, in one picture, so as not to leave untold ideas behind, because reading your comment below I realized that not all that I conceived is the same for another person. ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71871141/216837635-bf52dd6c-25b0-4a39-8ce7-072f3f2aeec5.png) @dwelle Thanks for the feedback ! author: anatoliibakhtin

body:
@anatoliibakhtin I love your designs!

That said,  the (4)/(5)/(6) are not obvious to me. I could easily explain the (4.1) to both mean either "send to back" or "send backward". And I don't think that (3.1) looking noisy is the issue — it's actually conveying that the selected element (purple) will be hidden behind all the other elements.

I agree on the ordering though.

So we should:

1. go back to using colors
2. order as: `send to back` `send backward` `send forward` `send to front`

body: Possibly the confusion is that holding the spacebar sets the cursor to [grabbing](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/cursor#:~:text=Something%20is%20being%20grabbed) rather than just [grab](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/cursor#:~:text=Something%20can%20be%20grabbed), making people think it's already started grabbing. Possibly changing it so that it starts in grab mode when you hold the spacebar, and switches to grabbing mode when you press on the mouse could fix this confusion?

body:
I guess let's do it the "customize everything" for now?

1. if `<HamburgerMenu>` not rendered from host app, default to something like this:

    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/208889540-5eb7e8b5-1b13-4a58-bf1e-a229a7e3ce55.png)

2. if `<HamburgerMenu>` rendered from host app, don't render any items inside of it, and require host apps to build it up from the default exported items and their own items.
author: dwelle

body: Yep agreed 👍🏻
author: ad1992

body:
To start out:

1. If `<HamburgerMenu>` not rendered, render the default.
2. If rendered, render it empty.
3. Create a `<HamburgerMenu.Item>` component that we'll use for our own hamburgerMenu items, which we'll wrap into their own components and export publicly for people to reuse. And we'll also export the `<HamburgerMenu.Item>` itself so people can build their own custom items.
4. The `<HamburgerMenu.Item>` should take `icon?: React.Node`, `onClick?: () => any`, `shortcut?: string` , and `children` props. `children` being the text or whatever else people want to render. 
5. Also export a `<HamburgerMenu.Separator>`

Example:

```jsx
import { Excalidraw, HamburgerMenu } from "@excalidraw/excalidraw";

return (
  <Excalidraw>
    <HamburgerMenu>
      <HamburgerMenu.ItemOpen/>
      <HamburgerMenu.ItemExportFile/>
      <HamburgerMenu.ItemExportImage/>
      <HamburgerMenu.ItemHelp/>
      <HamburgerMenu.ItemResetCanvas/>
      <HamburgerMenu.Item icon={<SomeIcon/>} onClick={() => console.log("Heisenberg")}>
        Say my name.
      </HamburgerMenu.CustomItem>
      <HamburgerMenu.Separator/>
      <HamburgerMenu.ItemThemeToggle/>
      <HamburgerMenu.ItemCanvasBackground/>
    </HamburgerMenu>
  </Excalidraw>
)
```
author: dwelle

body: In addition, it's better to keep it "Menu" instead of "HamburgerMenu" to keep it short and simple.
author: ad1992

body: Perhaps, though we need to ensure the naming of all the components we'll end up with is clear and distinguishable. Menu is pretty generic (but so is HamburgerMenu).

body:
From the images above it _may_ be that the canvas size exceeded the database limit of 1MB. For some stupid reason we're not showing an error message on these failures :(. Nonetheless, if a save fails, it shouldn't allow you to close the browser tab as it will prompt you that you may have unsaved data.

@nikolra @marcelatrujillo-takeblip Could you check your file sizes as of _now_? You can do so by right-clicking the canvas, opening "Stats for nerds" and checking the total storage size:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/210283270-e0031971-daa8-4851-89d0-52184acaab36.png)

If it was a save failure, then there's no way to retrieve lost data, sorry :(. But even otherwise, it's likely the data weren't saved properly. The only case where we could retrieve the data is if the shapes were marked as deleted and the save happened within the last 24 hrs.

I've merged a fix to show the errors properly: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6063

body: Hi. The npm package does not include any tracking, it's used just for the excalidraw.com app!
author: dwelle

body: But what if I need to edit the code itself? Is there a branch from which the docker image (https://hub.docker.com/r/excalidraw/excalidraw) gets built?
author: RobinSrimal

body:
No branch. The docker image is built uses a modified build. But now I'm not sure whether it was being stripped properly (I wasn't on the Docker team nor do I use it), or whether it regressed.

In any case, if you plan to fork the repo to make changes, and you still want to be deploying the excalidraw.com app wrapper as opposed to build your app from the package API, then I suggest you delete the tracking code in index.html, or use the `REACT_APP_DISABLE_TRACKING=true` ENV var when building which we're adding to be sure: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6059.

We're filtering out non-excalidraw hostname tracking data regardless, so not much would happen.

body:
@zsviczian from https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin did an awesome work implementing basic search functionality by means of custom tools. Huge thanks for that!

I wonder if this feature could be transferred to Excalidraw as well, since on large whiteboards you might easily forget where you've mentioned certain things and a search function would come very handy in such situations.

The current search implementation in the Obsidian plugin shows all results at once, which can be overwhelming on bigger whiteboards, because all the results are so zoomed out to fit on the whole screen, so you can't really see the search results in detail. This could be solved by having a button and/or keyboard shortcut to jump to the next search result, like "ctrl+g" (which is commonly used in other apps for that), which will show only one search result zoomed in (like "shift+2" does now when an object is selected). Another press of "ctrl+g" should jump to the next search result and so on, while "shift+ctrl+g" would jump to the previous search result.

Ideally, a pop-up should show the count of all search results, and when jumping from one result to another this count should change, like from 1/8 to 2/8 and so on.
author: sojusnik

body:
Indeed, when switching in between search results, it should zoom into each one. Also particular result might be a very long text, and in this case it's important highlight the exact match line and column.

In my implementation, it's actually adding two temporary text elements ">" and "<" at the position of match. What do you think could be better highlighing solution?

Also, in my case, search is triggered by selecting any text element and pressing search hotkey, that triggers search across elements content of selected element as a search phrase. Do you think it's viable alternative to normally entering search phrase to input field?
author: 7flash

body:
> In my implementation, it's actually adding two temporary text elements ">" and "<" at the position of match. What do you think could be better highlighing solution?

Maybe just a simple yellow overlay would be enough, something like:
![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1245718/230713910-a45103eb-7efd-4aae-9baa-48be9f0c7c16.png)

> Also, in my case, search is triggered by selecting any text element and pressing search hotkey, that triggers search across elements content of selected element as a search phrase. Do you think it's viable alternative to normally entering search phrase to input field?

I think both are valid and useful ways to search, i.e. either press a hotkey and then type your search query or select one or several words in a text element and then press the hotkey to initiate the search (if I understood you correctly).

author: lpers

body:
To confirm If I understand correctly, rn since we are allowing only named components, host cannot render anything apart from that and hence the dialog inside collab will not have access to the context, atoms.
To solve this, if we have named `collab` component host will be able to render it. But for other components (custom ones) this is not possible as we filter. 
So one solution is what you are doing but in this we are allowing all top level children to render. Another approach what I think is for those components which could be used outside Excalidraw (so the generic UI components), eg button, dropdown, dialog, these should be pure components and not be dependent on these hooks directly, that way these components could be used outside excal easily and later on if we create a library of UI components of Excalidraw, these components could be directly moved into the library as they are pure components. In other words, these UI components shouldn't be tightly coupled to editor.
author: ad1992

body:
> To confirm If I understand correctly, rn since we are allowing only named components, host cannot render anything apart from that and hence the dialog inside collab will not have access to the context, atoms.
> To solve this, if we have named `collab` component host will be able to render it. But for other components (custom ones) this is not possible as we filter.

No, we're just not rendering anything custom from the host. Naming or not naming will not solve it.

> So one solution is what you are doing but in this we are allowing all top level children to render. Another approach what I think is for those components which could be used outside Excalidraw (so the generic UI components), eg button, dropdown, dialog, these should be pure components and not be dependent on these hooks directly, that way these components could be used outside excal easily and later on if we create a library of UI components of Excalidraw, these components could be directly moved into the library as they are pure components. In other words, these UI components shouldn't be tightly coupled to editor.

The Dialog in question is a private component, so excalidraw.com shouldn't even be using it — but it is (meaning, current production is buggy in that the state is not working as intended). We can rewrite the component's API so it's more reusable, but who knows how long that will take. And I'm not confident that we would be able to remove all dependencies on editor state — let's say dark mode toggling or i18n. So it wouldn't be reusable without exposing some Excalidraw provider/context as we discussed and making hosts wrap those components in that provider anyway — at which point whether you render inside the Excalidraw container or in the Excalidraw provider will not make that much difference.

author: maielo

body:
Notes on the updated API.

- The sidebar trigger is now a `<SidebarTrigger>` instead of `<Sidebar.Trigger>`, because now you should  be rendering it outside of the sidebar as it's not tied to a single one. It's also not conceptually inside the sidebar. And you may wanna render your custom sidebar conditionally for perf, but want to render the trigger always:

  ```jsx
  <Excalidraw>
    <SidebarTrigger>Custom trigger</SidebarTrigger>
    <Sidebar>
      {/* ... */}
    </Sidebar>
  </Excalidraw>
  ```

- We will support rendering multiple custom sidebars (as siblings). We get this for free with current implementation, and it will allow people to have multiple sidebars without having to use tabs if they don't want to:

  ```jsx
  <Excalidraw>
      <Sidebar name="custom">
        custom 1
      </Sidebar>
      <Sidebar name="custom2">
        custom 2
      </Sidebar>
  </Excalidraw>
  ```

- Each `<Sidebar/>` has to have a `name` prop which we use to identify it and render it based on state.

- The sidebar tabs are optional, so people can still render content without tabs into `<Sidebar.Content/>`.
author: dwelle

body: Shouldn;t name of `<SidebarTrigger>` be `<Sidebar.Trigger>` to align in with rest ? (i know it's outside, but namespace wise it should be same IMHO)
author: maielo

body:
> Shouldn;t name of `<SidebarTrigger>` be `<Sidebar.Trigger>` to align in with rest ? (i know it's outside, but namespace wise it should be same IMHO)

I'm afraid `<Sidebar.Trigger>` will make it seem you should use it inside `<Sidebar>` and that it belongs to a specific `<Sidebar>` component, whereas it's replacing the editor's sidebar (currently `library`) toggle.

And we may actually support multiple triggers (which would then be `<Sidebar.Trigger>`) which you would be able to render anywhere. E.g. on Plus the comment button could be a `<Sidebar.Trigger>`.

So perhaps we should rename it to `<SidebarMainToggle>`. Yeah, it's not great.

----

One thing I just realized is that we need to add support for rendering custom sidebar and merging the library sidebar into it as one of the tabs.
author: dwelle

body:
### API for sidebar headers When rendering multiple tabs, you may want to have a different sidebar header for each tab. For example, a sidebar that contains `library` and `comments` tabs will have different headers for each. There are two ways to do this: - **API variant 1** Nest multiple headers as direct children of `` (as we do currently), where each header will have `tab` prop binding it to a specific tab. ```jsx Tab one custom header Tab two custom header

Custom sidebar

Tab one content Tab two content One Two ``` - **API variant 2** Nest each header inside the respective ``. **Pros:** - No need for binding through `tab` props, and the headers will be nested where they belong. This will also allow for having each tab as its own component with its own state, especially if the header depends on that state. - Actually should be easier to implement on our side. **Cons:** - slightly less semantic in that you will render it inside `TabContent` as sibling of its content, but it will be rendered outside the tab DOM. But, that's mostly an implementation detail. But, it will force Header position (on top) because we will render using tunnels. Currently, we allow rendering the header wherever you want. But I think it's fine. You will also be able to render the header outside of the `TabContent` if you want just one header per whole sidebar (all tabs). ```jsx

Custom sidebar

Tab one custom header Tab one content Tab two custom header Tab two content One Two ```
author: dwelle

body:
I'm also thinking of nuking the "render default header if no custom one supplied". Will remove tons of complexity. In other components we don't do that either, and make you render your content instead (e.g. `MainMenu`).

EDIT: the preventing 2+ headers at the same time we may actually keep for cases like adding custom tabs into default sidebar without having to render common header on top.
author: dwelle

body: Hey @ignacio-cuadra I am also working on some refactors/fixes related to text wrapping/bound containers so we can collaborate :) As per the `min width`, till now emojis were not considered, but the previous behavior should also be retained (min-width without emoji) so, in that case, let's default to without emoji. body:
Unfortunately, the Apple Pencil “double-tap” isn’t exposed to web pages, so Excalidraw running in a browser can’t detect it. Apple only provides this gesture via native iOS APIs ([UIPencilInteraction](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uipencilinteraction)).

Unless Excalidraw ships a native iOS wrapper that bridges the gesture into JS, this can’t be implemented in the web app. I suggest closing this issue.

body:
Correct, saving the file locally is only to solve the problem of "not losing data". It won't solve the size limit issue on the server. Refreshing still reconnects to the same collaboration room.

The constant message is not great, we should figure that out. Perhaps show once, and then show a status icon on canvas instead.
author: dwelle

body:
I understand the reason behind this error and for me it shows up at the correct moment – when excalidraw (free browser app) can't handle more data and I keep losing connection with my students – so the message itself isn't the key part of the problem. 

My current workaround is to use excalidraw in incognito and, after the error happens: export a picture → stop session → clear canvas → exit (and tell my student to do so too) → reopen and start a new session in new incognito window → send my student a new link.
(Maybe not every step is necessary, but I don't feel like checking everything during work).
Works, but it's less than ideal to do that every ~50–60 minutes, especially that my lessons are most often a clock hour long, just enough for it to break but not be worth of restarting. 

From my perspective even a gauge telling me how 'full' is my current board would be helpful – this way I can anticipate the break and move on with another exercise onto a new session.
This could be also solved by allowing me to clear canvas during the shared session – show a warning that I better export what currently is on the board, then you can free your server from my data and I can go on with my lesson.

Meanwhile any more convenient workaround would be appreciated, even if one of them would be to buy the product (but if I'm to pay, it would absolutely need to work), I like excalidraw and want to keep using it.
author: doa1996

body:
Hey @doa1996 — do you create a new collab room for every hour-long class? In other words, you are able to fill the canvas above limit (1MB) in one teaching session? If so, that is very strange, as only the shapes count towards the limit (images do not count).

As for a gauge, if you open the stats for nerds (right-click on canvas), the `storage total size` is a rough estimate.

Whatever I do, even now, the Storage stats remain the same on my end (scene – 2b; total – 1.1k). Strangely enough, after "empty cache and hard reload" through chrome devtools, I tested with two incognito windows on my own and tried to make as much mess as possible: over 500 elements, 20k width and 10k height, until my own PC started giving up – and the session remained intact. Maybe my students need to clear cache and use incognito too, we'll try things out in a few days and keep you updated.

body: @dwelle So I asked one of my students to clear their cache and hard reload, as I did myself before, and our first lesson after that wasn't interrupted by errors or performance issue; but the second lesson was. I guess for the time being I'll repeat the process with everyone and use excalidraw in incognito mode, as this is the simplest way to ensure cache not getting clogged. For the record, the Stats for nerds → storage never changed from scene = 2b, total = 1.1k. Again, I'm all for hosting an instance of the program myself or pay IF that ensures everything will work. I like your product, just wish it worked without me performing software acrobatics, and not everyone with such problems happens to be a programmer knowing how to mitigate them.

author: raqbit

body: Good afternoon! For people who liked the Excalidrow, it really lacks just a little bit of memory to work for 1 hour or 1 hour 20 minutes. I would suggest increasing "export const MAX_ALLOWED_FILE_BYTES = 2 * 1024 * 1024" by 10 times, or at least 5 times. And reduce the storage time "export const DELETED_ELEMENT_TIMEOUT = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000". Since the program is open source, in principle I can make these changes. Of course, I won't be doing this anytime soon, unless within my own server when I get my hands on it.

@dwelle To fix this, I'm thinking of only showing the maximum file exceeded error once as a popup and then having an icon at the bottom to state the error again on hover. This UI change should make it useable during the collab even if the data can no longer be saved to firebase, but the error icon on the bottom should still remind the user that the is a problem with the save, like this:

![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/82120596/6aabba17-924d-4b5b-b510-93b0fd4daa86)

The suggested method will only try to save to firebase once, and when it finds maximum size exceeded then it will no longer try to save.

![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/82120596/6c3c33b6-96c3-4aef-8a96-b467047363c6)

Will this implementation be satisfactory, or does not saving the collab room to Firebase result in any other problems in the rest of the code. If you think this implementation is fine, then I'm happy to try and do it.

body:
There are also many other parts of the UI that don't re-render after a language change. Maybe the best solution would be to reload the whole page after language change or make all of the text wrapped in the `t` function to change their language state when language state updates?

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/95122845/218280954-9c30f2ac-4ce5-4874-83e0-8b50548d435b.png)
author: LukaHietala

body:
Now this is really strange.. The link to the text is wrapped around with the i18n `t` function which updates all the text inside to the translated one when language is changed, but it doesn't seem to do it really well on some places.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/95122845/218282731-eea4d93f-94e4-45f9-a0e2-1fe94463badb.png)
author: LukaHietala

body:
The main problem is that "t" is used inside and outside of components. So we can't directly use a hook that gets the t and causes a re-render when it updates.

The current solution they have, according to my understanding, is to put the lang code in a Jotai atom that is used by the App parent component, update this atom from the language selection component (this causes a re-render in the parent App component), detect when the App component re-renders and update the language in i18n (the module responsible for providing "t") if the language is changed.

However, the problem with that is that some children components do not re-render because of children props or memoization. In that case, they do a hack, which is to make these children use the appState context. I think they update this appState context whenever the language changes. This way the children components re-render.

But everytime they do that, they comment above it saying that this is a hack that should be fixed. This is why I haven't opened a pull request.

This bug could be fixed by doing useAppState() in the footer component thing.

body: Debugging further found that the text element's `containerId` is set to `null` when received in `remote elements` during `reconciliation`. Though I am still checking why would that happen :/
author: ad1992

body:
In collab on sync we store the incoming remote elements, which let's say contains just the single `text` or `rectangle` element based on what changed. And the `restoreElements()` removes the binding in `repairContainerElement()`/`repairBoundElement()` because it thinks the container/bound element no longer exists as we don't pass those.

We'll need to repair bindings only on full `restore()` (init).
author: dwelle

body:
> In collab on sync we store the incoming remote elements, which let's say contains just the single `text` or `rectangle` element based on what changed. And the `restoreElements()` removes the binding in `repairContainerElement()`/`repairBoundElement()` because it thinks the container/bound element no longer exists as we don't pass those.
> 
> We'll need to repair bindings only on full `restore()` (init).

Yep found the same when debugging since the restoreElements don't contain the container so its setting to null in `repairBound` element. 

Will raise the PR.

body:
Really interesting idea @7flash, would love to see something like that. 

As a user of @zsviczian's Obsidian Excalidraw I was just thinking about a use case for layers for learning. By being able to embed an Excalidraw drawing in a note with different layers in place you basically have an excellent system for visual flashcards. 

For example you can have a layer that occludes part of the image as a question, and then the base layer which is unoccluded as the answer. Implementing this with layers means many different 'views' would be possible on a single drawing.

author: shuguang-lv

body:
Yup. Let's spec it out first though.

I'd not do selection order because that seems too cryptic / inconsistent, and doesn't solve box selection or select-all -> copy.

With spatial ordering, I'd do it as if reading a book:

![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/5153846/e2f191b2-b2c4-4f7d-b4da-72eeb25d3294)

More complex example:

![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/5153846/7f819176-38fd-4da7-ba7c-e0502d367e5b)

That said, that will work only for LTR, top-to-bottom languages. Perhaps we can change the algo based on active language setting. But let's start with the above.
author: dwelle

body: @7flash @dwelle Hi, thanks for your explanation. From my perspective, the ordering of copied elements in the text should follow the order by which they are selected and respect active language settings. Is my understanding correct?
author: shuguang-lv

body: @shuguang-lv hey. Actually, I'd implement the left-to-right, top-to-bottom approach as shown on the images. And disregard the active language for now.
author: dwelle

body:
Thinking about it more, the way I described (or rather not described) the spatial algorithm is too simplistic and wouldn't work well with common cases where text elements that people would normally consider on a single line would not be considered on the same line by the algo because the elements would not have the same `y` position (unless you always aligned all elements vertically):

![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/5153846/b2b90971-44ea-4347-96b3-256f90edcf95)

A better algo would be to take the top-most text element, compute its bounding box horizontal center line, and consider all elements whose bounding boxes touch that line to be on the same line with it. Then go left to right as normal. Afterwards, take the next top-most text element (of elements that are left) and do it again.

![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/5153846/a0d87ab4-87a7-45ef-897e-961dbed42b4e)

Above, the `seven` element is considered as the beginning (Y-position wise) of the third line. If we think that semantically it's better to be kept on line 2, we can also add some tolerance as well.

I came up with this just after a short amount of thinking. If anyone can suggest a better heuristic, feel free!

I asked GPT what it thinks, and it came up with a similar algo, though it's using tolerances instead:

![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/5153846/51452b64-dc35-4549-8d1e-fd2f0f343060)

---

Another thing to consider is the output we should copy to clipboard. Right now, we copy each text shape on its own line. We could concat all "single line" shapes in a single line separated by spaces, but maybe that's assuming too much on the user's part. So let's start with keeping the current behavior (each shape its own paragraph), and go from user feedback afterwards.
author: dwelle

body: @dwelle Thanks for your detailed explanation! It makes sense to me. I'll look into relevant algo
author: shuguang-lv

body: @dwelle Hi, may I ask what's the base point of an element when calculating its xy coordinates? i.e. top-left corner, bottom-right corner, or the centroid?
author: shuguang-lv

body:
`element.x` and `element.y` reflects the top-left corner.

To get the element bounds (top-left, bottom-right), including (center-x, center-y), you can use the `getElementAbsoluteCoords()` helper from `src/element/bounds.ts`.

body: Thank you. That's a great hint to me!

body: @dwelle Hi~ Regarding your solution 2 above, I have a question. What if texts are in significantly different sizes? Like the case below, "1" touches the center lines of both "4" and "3". Which two should we think are on the same line?
author: shuguang-lv

body:
I'd do `4 1 3`, which conforms to the suggested algorithm.

With edge cases like this it's hard. I can image we could do `4 3 1`, but it'd be hard to write a generic algo for it.

author: andrew-demb

body:
I attempted to reproduce this issue on 14/05/2026

Node.js: node -v 22.22.2
pnpm: pnpm -v  11.1.1
yarn: yarn -v (fill in) 1.22.22

Finding: Running pnpm install is now actively blocked by the "packageManager" field in the root package.json, which enforces yarn via corepack. The original TypeScript errors (TS2322, TS2339) can no longer be reproduced as they appear to have been resolved by other PRs (e.g. #6753).

`[ESLint] Found 0 errors and 0 warnings
[TypeScript] Found 0 errors. Watching for file changes.`

Given the original bug is no longer reproducible and pnpm support was never formally committed to, I'd suggest this issue can be closed. If pnpm support is still desired, it may be worth tracking as a separate dedicated issue.

body:
@ad1992, in [this specific case](https://excalidraw.com/#json=MRsjneUy5gIRKcIdLtNhR,RYoXFMds9L_nubHEPEWdSw), adding an offset of 30 almost solves the issue... of course, this means that the textElementWidth and viewportX calculations are offset for some reason, though I don't see why since that part of the code did not change.

```typescript
      Object.assign(editable.style, {
        font: getFontString(updatedTextElement),
        // must be defined *after* font ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
        lineHeight: `${lineHeight}px`,
        width: `${textElementWidth+30}px`,
        height: `${textElementHeight}px`,
        left: `${viewportX-30}px`,
```

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/8542c95a7a2b30d88b9a4f71867651fe60a4c7da/src/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L283-L290

body:
@savager by any chance do you have turned on "Aggressive Block Filtering" on Brave ?
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11256141/223698867-753140fb-5550-4743-b12b-0c0e857b93ce.png)

When this is turned on the canvas `measureText` doesn't work well and returns `0` due to which all text elements width is `set` to `0` and no way you can recover it unless you turn off the "Aggressive Block Filtering" on Brave

body:
I'd love this, too. Using a Surface Pro 7 in Firefox. It's pretty much the thing that keeps my team and I in OneNote TBH.

**Specific Behavior I'm Looking for:**

* if using the pen tip, use the current tool
* if using the eraser end, use the eraser _regardless of the currently selected tool and regardless of the lock setting_
* when no longer using the eraser end, revert to the otherwise selected tool _regardless of the lock setting_

Basically, the eraser end should always be able to invoke the eraser without changing what the pen tip will do when I use it again.

If you need some users with relevant hardware to test, please at me. Would love to help out!

**EDIT:** Took a peek at the source to see you're using the PointerEvent. I confirmed [on this MDN playground](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/play?id=1Zr0y9x7ZDTe7y0w7xpgNvl3AxElnXj51T8jEMYDUuJqcp3p3f7dF1NNz1Hl1Doxr%2F8oUJ8KmWYOMX%2FV) that my eraser has pointer type "pen" and button index "5". I only have my own hardware though. If other folks want to confirm if there's any consistency there, that'd be sweet.

author: ishaan1091

body:
Looking into the shortcuts, we actually need to reserve `alt-w` for "align vertically".

We could technically use `alt-shift-w`, but I'm worried that people will misremember it and press `cmd/ctrl-shift-w`. Even more so for `ctrl/cmd-alt-w`.

IMO we should come up with a different letter to `w`. Or use just `w` or `shift-w`, though that will preclude using the shortcut from within the text editor if we wanted to.
author: dwelle

body:
We can't use the shortcut directly from the text editor anyways as we need to have the element selected. So having only ```w``` or ```shift + w``` shouldn't impact or we can use the letter ```b``` 

Update - I have updated it to ```alt + b``` and ```alt + shift + b``` in the PR @dwelle
author: ishaan1091

body: I'm thinking we should have just a single shortcut for wrap/unwrap. Whether we should reuse it for bind/unbind is an open question. I'd side with yes. We may have `shift-<key>` on top to _always_ unwrap/unbind, but I'd keep the non-shift shortcut to do both, while preferring wrap/bind if a multi-selection contain unwrapped/unbound element(s). Though with binding, multiselection shouldn't be support (or we'd have to make it smart such as bind to nearest container).

body:
I like the idea! I have a question about what should happen if the return line makes the lasso self intersecting.

## All Good

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/78166995/225646825-61a06b2b-c6a1-47c7-91b1-0e1e263d5c55.png)

## Self-Intersecting

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/78166995/225647021-48f3e85e-1844-4d51-b84d-a7a5a4ac9e4a.png)

It seems obvious what the behavior should be in the first image, but what should the behavior be like in the second?
author: IlIllII

body:
I think this could be a good idea. The only thing to decide is how to expose this to the user without necessarily complicating the UI.

One way is to use a modifier key (or stylus button as suggested), such as Alt-key (/cc @MxMossy).

The other is to expose a UI switch, but I'd want to avoid that for now.

### implementation

@IlIllII for intersection, let's use the [even-odd](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Even%E2%80%93odd_rule) rule we for background fills. So in your example, the shape would be selected.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/233408875-9861bdcc-0681-4a20-8769-678641229c1b.png)

@LennartCode for now, I'd align with our current selection behavior of completely enclosing the shape for them to be selected (ad your second point). The edge cases of not wanting to select dots is actually a good use case for this lasso selection because you can lasso around them.
author: dwelle

body:
@dwelle Thanks, this seems good enough to start with.

What behavior should be expected when the user begins a regular box selection and presses Alt in the middle? Should the lasso selection start from the initial mouseDown point or from the moment alt is pressed?

body:
revisiting this.

@aidan-gibson no one working on this currently I think, /cc @MxMossy 

> What behavior should be expected when the user begins a regular box selection and presses Alt in the middle? Should the lasso selection start from the initial mouseDown point or from the moment alt is pressed?

Let's start the lasso selection only if alt is already being pressed at the point when `pointerdown`/`pointermove` happens.
